pr.title: Various easy_funcs, mostly Kirby funcs involving Yoshi
pr.author: Gelatart

summary: Matched a small batch of simple wrapper functions: eight Kirby copied-Yoshi SpecialN collision callbacks in ftKb_Init and one Corneria ground function. The Kirby functions all dispatch through existing fighter collision/state helper callbacks, while grCorneria_801E0DE4 was typed as returning s32 and forwards the Ground_GObj plus its Corneria ground-vars substruct into grCorneria_801E2550. The decomp.dev report confirms 9 new matches for +360 bytes and a +0.01% matched-code increase.
what_changed: Replaced eight ftKb_Init.c placeholder comments with matched Kirby copied-Yoshi collision functions: ftKb_YsSpecialN1_Coll, ftKb_YsSpecialAirNCapture2_Coll, ftKb_YsSpecialAirNCapture1_Coll, ftKb_YsSpecialNCapture1_Coll, ftKb_YsSpecialAirCapture2_Coll, ftKb_YsSpecialAirCapture1_Coll, ftKb_YsSpecialNCapture2_0_Coll, and ftKb_YsSpecialNCapture2_1_Coll.
Each matched Kirby collision function is a one-call wrapper around either ft_8008403C or ft_80082C74 with a corresponding local callback symbol fn_8010984C through fn_80109BF8.
Added prototypes in ftKb_Init.h for the callback functions fn_8010984C, fn_801098EC, fn_8010998C, fn_80109A08, fn_80109A84, fn_80109B00, fn_80109B7C, and fn_80109BF8.
Replaced the grCorneria_801E0DE4 placeholder with a matched implementation that obtains Ground* via GET_GROUND(gobj) and returns grCorneria_801E2550(gobj, &gr->gv.corneria).
Changed grCorneria_801E0DE4's prototype from void to s32 and changed grCorneria_801E2550 from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to s32 grCorneria_801E2550(Ground_GObj* gobj, struct grCorneria_GroundVars* corneria).
Added a forward declaration for struct grCorneria_GroundVars in src/melee/gr/forward.h so grcorneria.h can expose the typed grCorneria_801E2550 prototype.
smart_moves: Used existing high-level transition/collision helpers ft_8008403C and ft_80082C74 rather than trying to hand-expand identical low-level logic, which made the Kirby copied-Yoshi functions concise and matchable.
Kept unknown local callback names as address-based fn_8010xxxx symbols while still exposing their Fighter_GObj* signatures, avoiding speculative names while enabling typed calls.
For the Corneria match, first recovered enough type information to express the call as GET_GROUND(gobj) followed by &gr->gv.corneria, which clarified the data path and fixed the function return type.
Introduced only a forward declaration for grCorneria_GroundVars instead of pulling a heavier Corneria struct definition into forward.h.
decomp_lessons: Easy collision callbacks can often be matched as thin wrappers around common fighter helpers plus a per-action callback; search nearby action-state collision functions for the same ft_8008403C/ft_80082C74 pattern.
When a stage function forwards a field of Ground::gv to another helper, a precise helper prototype can unlock both the caller implementation and better header documentation.
Prefer address-based fn_ names for local helper callbacks when behavior is not yet understood; matching and typing can proceed without over-naming.
Header cleanup matters for matching: changing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete s32 and pointer parameters documents recovered ABI details and enables cleaner C at callsites.
Small batches of simple functions are valuable: this PR produced 9 new 100% matches, each 40 bytes, for +360 matched bytes.
naming_conventions: Kirby copied-Yoshi functions use ftKb_YsSpecial... names, with Air vs ground and Capture/NCapture state distinctions preserved in the symbol name.
Unidentified internal callbacks retain fn_8010xxxx address names in ftKb_Init.h.
Stage functions retain address-style grCorneria_801Exxxx names when semantic names are not known.
The recovered Corneria ground variable type is declared as struct grCorneria_GroundVars and passed as a pointer named corneria.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Match one-call fighter collision wrappers by selecting the correct helper: ft_8008403C for several grounded or non-air variants, and ft_80082C74 for several air/capture variants shown in the diff.
Use exact callback address symbols as function pointers/arguments to preserve call target identity.
For grCorneria_801E0DE4, recover the compiler shape by assigning Ground* gr = GET_GROUND(gobj) and then passing &gr->gv.corneria to grCorneria_801E2550.
Changing the callee prototype grCorneria_801E2550(Ground_GObj*, struct grCorneria_GroundVars*) made the caller expressible without UNK_PARAMS.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
decomp.dev bot reported GALE01 matched code increased to 39.05% (+0.01%, +360 bytes) with 9 new matches, all moving from 0.00% to 100.00%.
follow_up_queries: Find other ftKb_YsSpecial collision placeholder functions that are likely one-call wrappers around ft_8008403C or ft_80082C74.
Inspect fn_8010984C through fn_80109BF8 to determine whether any can receive semantic names beyond address-based fn_ symbols.
Search other stage files for functions that pass &gr->gv.<stage> into helper routines and can be typed similarly to grCorneria_801E2550.
Look for remaining grCorneria.h UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS prototypes near fn_801E2454, fn_801E2480, fn_801E24AC, grCorneria_801E2598, grCorneria_801E25C4, and grCorneria_801E2738.
